ScenarioDef.label: The Celestial Fox - Primitive
ScenarioDef.description:
Aethira Dawnforge wakes on a forgotten rimworld with nothing but her divine power and two Kurin followers who still believe in her. No technology. No shelter. No walls. Just a goddess, sticks, and stones - and the certainty that whatever finds them first will not find them weak.

Start with Aethira Dawnforge and 2 Kurin followers. Animal tech level.

(Tip: Pick the 'Demigodess Worship' preset ideology for the full experience. Aethira cannot truly die - she always comes back.)

scenario.summary: An immortal Kurin Demigodess leads two Kurin followers. Campfires, sticks, and stones.

li.text:
The stolen ship finally gave out three days ago. The glitterworld technology that once kept them alive in the void is now scrap metal in a crater somewhere behind them.
Aethira remembers the Republic. The markets, the food stalls, the mechanoid workshops. She remembers the siege, the burning farmlands, the screaming. She remembers being on the last ship out, carrying the wounded while Aolara shrank behind them.
Now there is only dirt, sky, and two Kurin who still believe in her. No technology. No walls. No weapons worth the name. Everything the Republic built across two centuries of civilization, gone. They start from nothing.
But Aethira has started from nothing before. She has died and come back more times than she can count. This rimworld does not know what walks upon it.
Let it learn.

ScenarioDef.label: The Celestial Fox - Medieval
ScenarioDef.description:
A hundred years after the crash, Aethira Dawnforge has wandered half the continent gathering scattered Kurin survivors. She has remembered enough of the Republic's craft to teach steel and stone again. Now she walks with two oath-sworn followers onto fresh soil to plant the foundation of a keep.

Start with Aethira Dawnforge and 2 Kurin followers. Pre-firearm tier. Pre-researched: Smithing, Complex Furniture, Stonecutting.

(Tip: Pick the 'Demigodess Worship' preset ideology for the full experience. Pairs well with Medieval Overhaul or VFE Medieval 2 if you want a deeper medieval simulation, but works fine on vanilla. Aethira cannot truly die - she always comes back.)

scenario.summary: An immortal Kurin Demigodess and two oath-sworn followers found a keep. Steel, smithing, and stonework. Pre-firearm.

li.text:
It has been a hundred years since the stolen ship fell from the sky.
In that time Aethira Dawnforge has walked half the continent. She has knelt at the graves of Kurin she could not save, and she has carried home the ones she could. She has set broken bones and bound burned hands. She has remembered, slowly, what the Republic's smiths once knew. Hammer and bellows. Tongs and quench. Walls of cut stone. Roofs of milled timber. The patient craft that came before the glitterworld.
Now she stands on fresh soil with two Kurin who have sworn their lives to her. They carry steel from her forge and oaths in their hearts. They will found a keep. They will train more smiths, more masons, more healers. They will build something the next siege cannot break.
The Dawnforge does not promise them peace. She promises them that she will be there when the dark comes for them. As she always has.

ScenarioDef.label: The Celestial Fox - Tribal
ScenarioDef.description:
Aethira Dawnforge arrives on a forgotten rimworld with two Kurin followers who have sworn their lives to her light. Bows, simple buildings, herbal medicine - and a goddess who has already lived for five centuries and has no intention of stopping now.

Start with Aethira Dawnforge and 2 Kurin followers. Neolithic tech level.

(Tip: Pick the 'Demigodess Worship' preset ideology for the full experience. Aethira cannot truly die - she always comes back.)

scenario.summary: An immortal Kurin Demigodess leads two Kurin followers. Bows, herbs, and faith.

li.text:
Centuries of drifting among the stars. Centuries of watching scattered Kurin communities rise and fall on rimworlds across the galaxy. Centuries of carrying the guilt of Aolara.
Aethira Dawnforge remembers the Republic. She remembers the two centuries of peace, the crafty business-foxes, the delicious food, the mechanoid workshops. She remembers the tariffs, the protests, the siege. She remembers one million becoming ten thousand.
Now she stands on new soil with two Kurin who still believe. They have seen her bleed and rise again, seen her mend the broken with a touch. They do not follow out of duty. They follow because they believe she can build something worth protecting.
The divine shrine must be raised. The colony must grow. And when darkness comes, and it will come, she will meet it as she always has.
With patience. With presence. With the certainty that she cannot die.
